description_long: This object is an antique-style shotgun, featuring a wide, flared metal barrel with ornate engravings and a dark, weathered finish. The receiver and lock mechanism are intricately detailed, with visible hammers and a trigger guard, suggesting a flintlock or percussion cap design. The stock is made of dark wood, shaped ergonomically for shoulder support, and shows signs of age and use. The grip area is wrapped or textured for improved handling, and the overall construction appears robust and heavy. The barrel is single and notably short and wide, typical of historical blunderbuss shotguns. Metal components dominate the upper structure, while the lower section is primarily wood, giving the object a classic, historical appearance.
description: An antique, wide-barreled shotgun with ornate metalwork and a dark wooden stock, featuring a robust, historical design. The object combines engraved metal and aged wood for a classic, heavy appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wide, flared muzzle of the barrel and the symmetrical alignment of the upper mechanism.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the ornate engravings on the barrel, the detailed lock mechanism, and the full wooden stock.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the end of the wooden stock and the alignment of the barrel and action components.
description_view_3: Opposite side view displaying the lock mechanism, trigger guard, and the curvature of the wooden grip and stock.
attribute_barrel_configuration: single-barrel, wide and flared with no visible ribbing or venting
attribute_trigger_guard_design: rounded, medium-sized trigger guard with a smooth contour and no visible finger grooves
